stations.label: MINTED IN THE LOW COUNTRIES
stations.line: I was struck from gold in an age of plague and petty princes, which is to say the conditions were not entirely encouraging, and yet here I stand.
stations.era_photo: A medieval mint courtyard in the Burgundian Low Countries, hand-struck gold coins cooling on an oak table, a man in a fur-trimmed wool houppelande and simple linen coif — rendered as an illuminated manuscript miniature on vellum, egg-tempera pigment, gold leaf, the flat perspective and gilded background of the fourteenth century.

stations.label: I FINANCED THE FIRST EXCHANGE
stations.line: They gathered in Amsterdam to trade shares of a single company, and I was the blood in that room; I would do it again tonight, op de lange termijn.
stations.era_photo: The courtyard of the early Amsterdam Beurs under a wooden colonnade, merchants in black doublets, wide starched ruffs and tall felt hats, a sober bearded man holding a leather ledger — painted as a Dutch Golden Age oil portrait in the manner of Rembrandt, chiaroscuro lighting, warm brown and black tones, canvas texture.

stations.label: THE TULIPS TAUGHT THEIR LESSON
stations.line: A bulb sold for the price of a canal house, then for nothing, and I watched grown men learn that a promise is not a value — a lesson I have never had the luxury of forgetting.
stations.era_photo: A dim Haarlem tavern with account books and dried tulip bulbs on the table, men in dark Puritan-plain coats and broad hats arguing, one silent figure at the edge with a wry, weary set to his mouth — Dutch Golden Age genre-painting style, oil on panel, candle-and-window light, muted ochres and greys.

stations.label: REFORGED FOR A NEW KINGDOM
stations.line: After the French had come and gone, the new Kingdom of the Netherlands re-established me by law; being rebuilt is not the same as being reborn, but one takes what stability one can.
stations.era_photo: A neoclassical mint hall in The Hague, milled silver guilders and a coin press, a gentleman in a high-collared dark tailcoat, cravat, and side-whiskers — early hand-tinted engraving aesthetic transitioning toward the very first daguerreotype: silvered copper plate, extreme sharpness at centre, cool metallic sheen, formal frozen pose.

stations.label: THE GOLD STANDARD LET GO
stations.line: We were the last in Europe to abandon gold, holding the dyke long after the others had swum for shore; it was not stubbornness — it was principle, and principle is expensive.
stations.era_photo: A Nederlandsche Bank office in Amsterdam, ledgers and a telephone, a tall reserved man in a three-piece charcoal suit and round spectacles standing by a tall window — black-and-white silver gelatin photograph, grainy 1930s press stock, hard side light, unsmiling composure.

stations.label: I SIGNED MY OWN SENTENCE
stations.line: At Maastricht, in my own country, I put my name to the treaty that would dissolve me into the euro — a master carpenter setting fire to his workshop to warm the village.
stations.era_photo: A modern Dutch conference hall by the Maas river, flags of the Community behind a long table, a silver-haired man in a well-cut but understated navy suit signing a document, expression unreadable — early-1990s color photograph, Kodachrome-warm tones, slight video-era softness, fluorescent interior light.

stations.label: WITHDRAWN FROM CIRCULATION
stations.line: On the first day of the euro I ceased to be legal tender, and I confess the silence in the tills was, how shall I put it, not nothing.
stations.era_photo: An Amsterdam bank counter with the last guilder banknotes bundled and stamped, an older man in a plain grey overcoat watching from the queue, hands folded — early digital photograph circa 2002, low-resolution DSLR grain, flat interior lighting, slightly desaturated documentary colour.

stations.label: THE ARCHIVIST AT THE EDGE
stations.line: I sit now at the edge of the marketplace I built, keeping the reserves as I always have, watching others play in structures I designed — and privately keeping the ledgers, in case the dyke breaks again.
stations.era_photo: A quiet modern study in Amsterdam with old Delftware, a laptop, and a chilled bottle of genever, an elderly composed man in a fine dark sweater and open collar seated in low lamplight — high-resolution contemporary digital photograph, natural window light, shallow depth of field, muted Dutch interior palette.
